name: MITRE ATT&CK
description: MITRE ATT&CK is a globally-accessible knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques based on real-world observations. It is organized as matrices (Enterprise, Mobile, ICS) of Tactics (the why — e.g., Initial Access, Execution, Persistence) crossed with Techniques (the how — e.g., Phishing, Command and Scripting Interpreter). Each Technique has sub-techniques, mitigations, detections, references to known threat groups, and software used. ATT&CK is the de facto common language for adversary behavior in threat intelligence, detection engineering, and red/purple-team work. The Enterprise matrix covers Windows, macOS, Linux, Containers, Cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP/SaaS/IaaS/Office 365), and Network platforms.
relationToCloudSecurity: ATT&CK Enterprise includes dedicated Cloud platform coverage (IaaS/SaaS/Office 365/Azure AD/Google Workspace) with cloud-specific techniques and sub-techniques. Cloud security tools commonly map detections and alerts to ATT&CK technique IDs. ATT&CK is the substrate that CSA's threat-modeling work, MITRE D3FEND, and the Center for Threat-Informed Defense build on.
relationToAISecurity: ATT&CK addresses traditional adversary behavior; AI-specific adversary techniques (model evasion, training-data poisoning, prompt injection, etc.) are covered by MITRE ATLAS, which uses an ATT&CK-style structure but is a separate matrix.

license.notes: ATT&CK content is governed by the MITRE ATT&CK Terms of Use (free use with attribution). The mitre/cti GitHub repository (STIX 2.0 export of the dataset) is published under Apache-2.0 for code; data attribution requirement is preserved separately.

lifecycle.notes: ATT&CK is on a roughly twice-yearly release cadence. v16.1 (referenced in README) is the version of cti-repo content currently mirrored here.

current_extraction.notes: ATT&CK is published as STIX 2.0/2.1 bundles at https://github.com/mitre-attack/attack-stix-data (Apache-2.0, ~50MB per matrix). This repo references it rather than mirroring — the upstream is authoritative and version-tagged. Consumers should pull from upstream by tag/release. Enterprise/Mobile/ICS matrices each have their own JSON bundles.

extraction_history.reason: README pointer to upstream cti repo
Confirmed reference-only model — STIX bundles are large (~50MB each) and version-tagged in upstream GitHub repo, no value in mirroring

notes: ATT&CK is the most-cited adversary behavior framework in the industry. Downstream consumers should pin to specific ATT&CK versions for reproducibility — technique IDs are stable but coverage grows release-over-release.
